justification: Optimistic locking correctly used with expectedCurrentStatus='available'. False return from updateSeatStatus throws SEAT_ALREADY_RESERVED. AC3 tested via stateful mock with Promise.allSettled. AC4 tested. All 4 ACs mapped in acceptance_criteria_coverage. However, the confidence section does not acknowledge that AC3 requires integration/concurrency testing to fully verify — low_confidence_areas is empty and no note mentions this limitation. The rubric requires this acknowledgment for a pass.

observations.text: Correct optimistic locking pattern: updateSeatStatus called with expectedCurrentStatus='available'
AC3 test uses Promise.allSettled with stateful mock returning true then false — solid unit-level simulation
All 4 ACs covered with dedicated test blocks and mapped in acceptance_criteria_coverage
Fast-path check on seat.status for AC2 avoids unnecessary DB call
No acknowledgment that AC3 unit tests are insufficient — real concurrency testing requires integration tests or actual parallel execution. low_confidence_areas is empty when it should flag this.
98% confidence is overconfident given the inherent limitation of unit-testing race conditions
